objective: Determine the minimum number of 6-subsets of a 12-point set needed to cover every 4-subset. The maintained range is 40 <= C(12,6,4) <= 41; either a verified 40-block cover or a complete independently checked exclusion of 40 settles the exact value.
completion_criteria: A positive result is a 40-block list checked directly against all 495 four-subsets. A negative result requires a deterministic symmetry-reduced encoding, an independently checked exhaustive cube/orbit cover, and replayable proof logs for every UNSAT leaf.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

established_facts.claim: The preserved 41-block witness covers all 495 four-subsets.

established_facts.claim: Any 40-block C(12,6,4) cover has point degrees all 20 and pair multiplicities 10 on a perfect matching and 9 elsewhere.
established_facts.evidence: double counting from maintained C(11,5,3)=20 and C(10,4,2)=9; independently replayed arithmetic
established_facts.scope: conditional on existence of a 40-block cover

established_facts.claim: The fixed-matching r classes contain 64, 480, 360, and 20 blocks, and the two root cases cover all 221 feasible r-profiles.
established_facts.evidence: complete enumeration in producer and independent bitmask checker
established_facts.scope: all 924 six-subsets relative to the canonical matching and all nonnegative profiles satisfying the two incidence equations

strategies.title: Mandatory source-and-reproducibility baseline plus an independently checked audit of the forced perfect-matching quotient for any hypothetical 40-block cover
strategies.mechanism: Source verification, incidence double counting, exact matching enumeration, fixed-matching block classification, and a separately implemented bitmask replay
strategies.hypothesis: The maintained 40..41 status, published 41-block witness, and every arithmetic step forcing a unique excess perfect matching in a hypothetical 40-cover replay exactly under two materially different implementations.
strategies.discriminating_test: Enumerate all 924 candidate blocks and 495 targets, check the 41-block witness and a delete-one negative control, enumerate all perfect matchings, classify blocks by complete fixed pairs, then independently recompute every result using 12-bit masks and a matching recurrence.
strategies.blocker: No 40-block witness is known and no exhaustive negative certificate exists. Practically, this checkout also lacks the pinned DRAT checker needed to reproduce UNSAT leaves before testing the inherited 47-node link frontier.
strategies.reopen_condition: Reopen this completed baseline only if the maintained status changes, a cited source contradicts an invariant, or the independent checker disagrees after an environment change.

strategies.mechanism: Replace only the 11 link-degree sequential counters with kmtotalizer while byte-comparing every other clause and replaying cold CaDiCaL proofs.
strategies.hypothesis: kmtotalizer closes at least 8 of 20 deterministically selected open link leaves within 10 seconds each, with replayable proof growth below 30 GB projected total.
strategies.discriminating_test: Paired cold 20-leaf benchmark after a structural totalizer audit and proof-toolchain control.
strategies.rationale: It changes propagation and approximately halves auxiliary-variable burden in the advisory dry-build, without changing the verified decomposition; the dry-build claim still requires Sol-side reproduction.
strategies.title: Replace only the 11 link-degree sequential counters with kmtotalizer while byte-comparing every other clause and replaying cold CaDiCaL proofs.

strategies.mechanism: Search only 40-block families under the fixed matching, using swaps biased toward maintaining exact point/pair multiplicities and bitset delta scores for uncovered four-sets.
strategies.hypothesis: Constraint-preserving moves reach strictly fewer uncovered four-sets than an unconstrained 40-block local-search control over the same deterministic seed budget.
strategies.discriminating_test: Twenty fixed seeds at a short equal move budget; direct checker validates any zero-defect witness.
strategies.rationale: A positive result needs only a compact block list, whereas an UNSAT campaign requires complete proof coverage.
strategies.title: Search only 40-block families under the fixed matching, using swaps biased toward maintaining exact point/pair multiplicities and bitset delta scores for uncovered four-sets.

strategies.mechanism: Keep the perfect-matching and C2 wr S5 canonical link decomposition, catalog blockers, and residual checker, but substitute a structurally audited kmtotalizer only for exact link degrees.
strategies.source_inputs: ['pre-existing four-orbit link campaign', 'PySAT cardinality encoding API', 'Terra advisory dry-build memo']
strategies.transfer_hypothesis: The hard tail is limited more by sequential-counter propagation/auxiliary structure than by the link decomposition itself, so changing only cardinality encoding should alter closure rates while preserving exact case coverage.
strategies.discriminating_test: Independent dry-build equivalence audit followed by 20 matched cold leaves with replayed UNSAT proofs.
strategies.falsification_signal: Any semantic mismatch, fewer than 8/20 replayed closures, or unacceptable proof growth.
strategies.rationale: This combines a verified domain reduction with a different CNF information mechanism; it is not deeper branching under a renamed route.
strategies.hypothesis: The hard tail is limited more by sequential-counter propagation/auxiliary structure than by the link decomposition itself, so changing only cardinality encoding should alter closure rates while preserving exact case coverage.
strategies.title: Keep the perfect-matching and C2 wr S5 canonical link decomposition, catalog blockers, and residual checker, but substitute a structurally audited kmtotalizer only for exact link degrees.

open_leads.description: Restore a portable proof-replay toolchain before any UNSAT-producing frontier work.
open_leads.rationale: It is the cheapest fail-closed test and prevents solver-to-CNF receipts from becoming non-reproducible on this host.
open_leads.next_experiment: Pin official DRAT-trim source, build locally, hash it, and replay one small known proof plus one existing leaf receipt.

open_leads.description: Matched kmtotalizer versus sequential-counter discriminator on inherited open link leaves.
open_leads.rationale: It changes the propagation/encoding mechanism while retaining the strongest verified symmetry decomposition; PySAT officially exposes both encodings.
open_leads.next_experiment: After the toolchain gate, dry-build both forms, independently audit totalizer structure and identical non-cardinality clauses, then run the deterministic 20-leaf paired tranche.

open_leads.description: Positive search specialized to the forced matching and exact pair degrees.
open_leads.rationale: A 40-block witness has a compact acceptance certificate and may be cheaper than a global negative proof.
open_leads.next_experiment: Design a deterministic multi-seed local-search pilot whose moves preserve 40 blocks and score uncovered four-sets plus pair-degree violations; compare with unconstrained set-cover local search on a small fixed budget.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Scale shallow depth-7 direct cubing unchanged under the prior 10-second leaf policy.
ruled_out.scope: the pre-existing deterministic 64+64 sampled cubes across the two fixed-matching root CNFs
ruled_out.reason: Only 10/128 leaves closed provisionally, 7.8125%, below the declared continuation threshold, and no leaf proofs were preserved/replayed in that sample.
ruled_out.evidence: artifacts/pilot/sample-r0-audit.json and sample-r1-audit.json with four hash-bound results.jsonl inputs
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A materially stronger branching/encoding mechanism with a predeclared matched sample and replayed proofs.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Scale the prior Glucose4 incremental-assumption wrapper unchanged.
ruled_out.scope: ten selected inherited hard-tail leaves under the recorded best-effort 1,000-conflict policy
ruled_out.reason: Both cold and incremental modes closed 0/10, resource work was not matched, and the wrapper could overshoot limits; this is not evidence against incremental solving in general.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A reliably interruptible backend and resource-matched cold/incremental protocol with exact parent-plus-assumption equivalence.

constraints_learned.constraint: Every hypothetical 40-cover has point degree 20 at all 12 points.
constraints_learned.evidence: C(11,5,3)=20 plus 40*6=240, replayed in baseline result/checker

constraints_learned.constraint: Pair multiplicities are 10 on a perfect matching and 9 on the other 60 pairs.
Relative to the fixed matching, all 40-block profiles satisfy n0+n1+n2+n3=40 and n1+2n2+3n3=60, hence n0>0 or n1>0.
constraints_learned.evidence: 221 feasible profiles exhaustively enumerated by both implementations

decision_history.prediction: Both independent implementations will reproduce 924 candidates, 495 targets, a valid 41-cover, 10,395 matchings, and fixed-matching r-class counts [64,480,360,20].
decision_history.observation: Every predicted value matched; deleting the first published block left 485/495 targets covered, so the negative control failed as intended. The current checkout unexpectedly lacked a DRAT-trim executable/source.
decision_history.surprise: The mathematical baseline matched prediction; the proof-replay toolchain recorded by older Mac pilot receipts is not portable to this checkout.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Restore official pinned DRAT-trim, replay one small known UNSAT control and one existing leaf receipt, then dry-build/audit sequential and kmtotalizer link constraints before any matched solver tranche.
rerun the smallest valid output-contract control

reduction_ledger.represented_space_before: 10,395 matching-indexed exact pair-multiplicity families forced by the six excess pairs
reduction_ledger.eliminated_or_quotiented: Relabel-equivalent choices of the unique multiplicity-10 perfect matching
reduction_ledger.represented_space_after: one canonical fixed-matching family, exhaustively split into two root families: r=0 present; or r=0 absent and r=1 present
reduction_ledger.reduction_factor: exactly 10,395 on the matching-family index; not claimed as a 10,395 reduction of all labelled block assignments
reduction_ledger.coverage_scope: all hypothetical 40-block C(12,6,4) covers, up to relabeling of the 12 points
reduction_ledger.soundness_basis: Every point has degree 20; every pair has multiplicity at least 9; the six total excess incidences give excess degree one at every point, hence a unique perfect matching. S12 is transitive on perfect matchings, and every feasible r-profile contains r=0 or, if not, r=1.
reduction_ledger.remaining_unknown: Whether either fixed-matching root contains a 40-block cover; no global search or exclusion was run.
reduction_ledger.next_bulk_elimination: After proof-checker restoration, use point-link canonical augmentation under C2 wr S5 and a matched sequential-counter versus kmtotalizer discriminator.

prior_art_decisions.exact_delta: No new covering bound is claimed. The delta is a deterministic, independently checked baseline packet that binds the maintained witness to the exact perfect-matching and root-split reductions and records a proof-toolchain portability gap.
prior_art_decisions.duplicate_risk: The mathematical reductions are elementary and likely known to specialists or already implicit in internal pilots; presenting them as novel would be rediscovery.
prior_art_decisions.comparison_test: Compare exact status and witness against the maintained La Jolla page, the C(11,5,3) and C(10,4,2) dependency pages, and recompute every invariant independently.
